meta.title: Per-step both-role figure charts (WDSF-first, researched)
meta.note: Real International Standard technique, charted per figure from the official WDSF Technique Books (2nd ed., May 2013) as the primary source (owner-provided copies; cell-level transcription, adversarially verified against the page images); dancecentral.info remains the source for figures the books do not contain. NO fabrication: figures without a verifiable per-step source were removed from the catalog rather than guessed. Each entry records its source. Turn amounts are recorded on the step row where the book prints them (WDSF prints 'x between N-1 and N' on row N, so alignment(N) = alignment(N-1) + turn(N)). Shared rise is taken from the Man's Rise-and-Fall column; a materially different Lady rise is recorded in the entry note. The books' Rotation and head-position ('Extension') columns are transcribed VERBATIM into each role's `rotation`/`head` fields as PROVENANCE ONLY (owner decision 2026-07-10, with D33: `turn` is the canonical rotation and the prose columns are not modelled, so the generator emits NEITHER; do not delete these cells — the rotation prose documents the body-turn grading behind the non-derivable alignments); rotation cells mentioning 'with shoulders' additionally map to bodyActions CBM. Role-asymmetric charts keep every printed step: the count grid is the union of both roles' printed beats, and a count one role does not dance simply has no entry for that role. The only WDSF column not modelled is Tango's 'Sudden Movement' (pressure). Regenerate packages/domain/src/figure-charts.generated.ts with scripts/gen-figure-charts.mjs. Further conventions: 'Com./Cont. to turn' prose carries no token and no note (implied by the following step's amount); a cell printing several amounts ('X then Y (Pivot/Swivel)') carries their SUM plus a verbatim note; 'X or Y' alternatives keep the directly-charted amount plus a verbatim note; a cell spanning several rows ('between/on/over steps N and M') is recorded once, on the span's last row; a fraction printed without L/R takes its direction from the partner's cell for the same step; body-turn-only cells become notes; placement qualifiers (without weight, part weight, brush, heel pull, toe pivot, shoulder leading) and unusual printed foot actions are echoed into the entry note; foot annotations '(LF)/(RF)' are dropped (feet are never stored). Alignment (2026-07-10): entryAlignment is the figure's START alignment; the exit is DERIVED (entry + the leader's summed turns, packages/domain/src/alignment.ts), so exitAlignment is stored ONLY for figures the derivation cannot reproduce (foot-vs-body turn splits, pointing/promenade endings — see docs/seed/alignment-derivation-report.md); an exitAlignment that derivation reproduces exactly is redundant and must be omitted (enforced by packages/domain/src/alignment.test.ts).

figures.corroboration: Transcribed cell-by-cell from the dancecentral.info chart (raw HTML fetched; Man + Lady rows). Consistent with the already-charted WDSF twin 'telemark' (foxtrot): same 3-step telemark shape with 3/4 total L turn for the leader and Lady heel turn on 2; dancecentral additionally prints a 4th step (RF fwd in CBMP, OP, S) as the closed finish.
figures.note: ISTD Silver (source: 'Silver Level figure'). Step 1: both roles 'start to turn L' (no amount printed on row 1). Step 2 leader turn printed 'slightly less than 3/8 between 1-2' -> recorded three_eighth_L. Step 3 leader turn printed 'slightly over 3/8 between 2-3, body turns less'; follower step 3 '3/8 between 2-3, body turns less'. Step 2 follower: heel turn (LF closes to RF); Lady rise differs: step 1 'rise slightly e/o 1, NFR', step 2 'continue to rise on 2' (Man prints 'Up'). Step 4: no rise printed for either role ('--') -> rise omitted. Position is printed only on step 4 ('in CBMP, OP' Man / 'in CBMP' Lady) -> unprinted rows omit position. Step 4 alignment printed 'DW' without qualifier; exitAlignment qualifier 'facing' inferred from the fwd step (flagged). Follower step 2 alignment printed 'LOD' (heel turn).

figures.corroboration: Fetched three times with differently-worded prompts; all passes agree on 4 step rows, all-Slow timing, footwork, sway and the 1/4 L turn spanning steps 1-3. Confirmed the page charts the figure for quickstep (Bronze), so no foxtrot transplant was needed.
figures.note: ISTD Bronze (per page). The page's chart has NO Rise & Fall column, so rise is omitted on every step. Turn is printed once on the step-2 row as '1/4 to L btwn 1-3' (spans steps 1-3) — recorded on step 2 as printed, not split per step. Step 3 is a close without weight for both roles. Step 1 'starts to turn left' is commencement text, not a turn amount. Lady step 2 footwork printed 'T, IE of TH'. Verifier re-confirmed the page is a QUICKSTEP chart: title 'Quickstep Change of Direction', quickstep URL path, 'Bronze Level figure, listed as an additional step that can be danced in Medal Tests' — not the foxtrot figure. One nit on the note wording: the chart rows DO carry a trailing rise cell, printed '--' (empty) on every step, rather than the column being absent — outcome unchanged, rise correctly omitted. Step 2 placement text prints 'R side leading' (leader) / 'L side leading' (follower); kept as descriptive text, not a bodyAction, consistent with the page marking CBM only on steps 1 and 4.

Rise from Man's column: 'Rise e/o 2' recorded as commence on step 2; step 3 printed 'Up. Lower e/o 3' recorded as lowering. Step 1 has no rise cell. Man step 2 is a Heel Turn (footwork HT). Lady step 3 printed 'RF diag fwd (brushed to LF)'. One fetch pass omitted the CBM mark on Man step 1; the targeted raw dump confirmed it, so it is recorded. Man step 4 alignment cell prints just 'DW' (no facing/backing qualifier printed); exitAlignment qualifier 'facing' matches a forward step but the qualifier itself is not printed. Verifier confirmed the lady's step 3 turn cell also prints 'body turns less' ('3/8 between 2-3, body turns less') — not just one fetch pass; and the man's step 2 alignment prints 'almost BLOD' (the 'almost' qualifier is not expressible in the alignment enum).

figures.note: ISTD silver. Chart printed with 11 rows: row 11 (RF fwd in CBMP, OP) is the first step of the following figure taken outside partner - the WDSF twin charts 10 steps. Lady rise: NFR on steps 1 and 5 (backing entries into turns). Step 4 is a pivot (Man LF back footwork THT, Lady RF fwd HTH, 1/2 to R on 4). Lady step 6 is a Heel Turn (RF closes to LF, footwork HT, 1/2 between 5-6). Turn cells without charted amounts: Man 5 and 8 'continue to turn'; Lady 3 'body completes turn', 8 'starts to turn' - recorded here, not as values. Rise omitted on steps 4 and 11 (cells blank). Side leading printed: Man 7 'R side leading', Man 10 'L side leading preparing OP'; Lady 7 'L side leading preparing OP', Lady 10 'R side leading'. Steps 9-11 alignments are relative to the new LOD (corner). Man step 9 'RF to side and slightly fwd' mapped to side. Verifier: step-to-timing mapping re-checked row by row in two independent reads - the 11 printed rows carry S,Q,Q,S,S,S,S,Q,Q,S,S exactly as charted (pivot on step 4 = S; heel-turn group 5-7 = SSS; Running Finish 8-11 = QQSS). One note omission: Lady step 5 turn cell also prints 'continue to turn' (no charted amount), alongside the already-recorded Man 5/8 and Lady 3/8 uncharted-turn cells. All turn amounts, sways, CBM rows, side-leading cells, NFR on Lady 1 and 5, and blank rise on 4 and 11 match the source.

figures.note: ISTD gold. Tipsy TO R charted; Tipsy to L is the exact mirror on opposite feet. 3 steps, Q&Q (step 2 on the '&'). Step 2 'move LF halfway towards RF' (Lady RF towards LF) mapped to direction close, per the twins. Turn omitted: printed 'None, or up to 3/8 to R or L'. Rise cells verbatim: 'Slight rise on 1 and 2' / 'Lower e/o 2' / 'Down, with sharp flexing of knees' - mapped to commence/lowering/lowering matching the twins. Sway omitted on steps 2-3: the printed cells are compound ('L/S' then 'L/R' for Man; 'R/S' then 'R/L' for Lady) and could not be resolved to single values (the twin tipsy-to-r recorded them as Man to_L then to_R). Step 3 footwork verbatim: 'IE of T and IE of T of LF' (Lady: of RF). No alignments charted - figure is danced wherever the preceding figure ends. No CBM marked. Verifier: chart cells (Q&Q timing, side/halfway-close/side, T-T-'IE of T and IE of T' footwork, commence/lowering/lowering rise, unresolved compound sways omitted, turn 'None, or up to 3/8 to R or L' omitted) all match the source. One syllabus-note misquote: the page never says 'timing varies per preceding figure' - it prints the ALIGNMENT of step 1 as 'According to preceding figure'; the conclusion (no alignments charted) is nonetheless correct. Caveat on the mirror claim: the source's Tipsy to Left chart prints the Man's turn cell as 'None, or up to 3/8 to R' (no 'or L') and its sway letters are not mirrored, so it is a step-wise mirror rather than a cell-for-cell one; since only TO R is charted here, no shipped cell is affected.

figures.corroboration: Fetched twice with differently worded prompts; transcriptions agreed. This is Method 1 from the page (the page carries Methods 1 and 2); the catalog already holds WDSF twins outside-swivel-method-1/2/3, and the Method 1 shape (back in CBMP with cross without weight, swivel to PP, fwd-and-across, side without weight) matches the method-1 twin.
figures.note: ISTD Silver. No sway column and no rise (tango). This is a swivel figure: on step 1 the leader's RF crosses in front of LF WITHOUT WEIGHT (ends in PP) and the follower's LF closes slightly back WITHOUT WEIGHT while she swivels to R; on step 3 both place the free foot to side small step WITHOUT WEIGHT. Turn amounts: leader step 1 printed 'Just under 1/8 to R, 1/8 to L between 1-2' - recorded eighth_R on step 1 (verbatim 'just under') and eighth_L on step 2; leader step 3 printed '1/8 to R'. Follower step 1 printed 'Just over 1/4 to R on 1' - recorded quarter_R (verbatim 'just over'); follower '3/8 to L between 2-3' recorded on step 3. Row 1 position: leader 'in CBMP', follower 'in CBMP, OP' - outside_partner used (the Lady's step is outside partner). Follower footwork verbatim: step 1 'HB (Foot flat) and IE of B of LF', step 2 'HB (Foot flat)'. Row 3 has no position printed. Entry alignment omitted: leader step 1 prints 'Between center and DC against the LOD toe turned in. End facing wall', which the alignment enum cannot express. Verified against the source 2026-07-15: Method 1 rows match cell-for-cell, including the verbatim footwork tokens ('BH with pressure on B of RF', 'HB (Foot flat) and IE of B of LF'), the 'just under 1/8 to R' / 'just over 1/4 to R' turn readings, the between-steps turns recorded on the completing step, and the without-weight foot placements. The page's unexpressible leader entry alignment ('Between center and DC against the LOD toe turned in') is correctly omitted.

figures.source: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/viennese-waltz/natural-to-reverse
figures.corroboration: Transcribed cell-by-cell from the dancecentral.info 'Natural To Reverse' chart (raw HTML fetched). Leader rows are the page's 'Lady/Man (LF Backward Change Step)' chart, follower rows its 'Man/Lady (RF Forward Change Step)' chart, per the page's printed note that after the first half of a Natural Turn the Man can perform the LF Backward Change Step while the Lady does the RF Forward Change. Matches the already-charted WDSF twin 'lf-backward-change-step-natural-to-reverse' on shape (back TH / diag back T / close BH-vs-TH, 1/8 R per turning step, entry backing LOD, exit backing DW); footwork on step 3 differs by source (dancecentral TH, WDSF BH), and the sources disagree on the backward dancer's sway direction (dancecentral to_L, WDSF to_R).
figures.note: Source labels this a Bronze figure (assignment lists ISTD Silver; source's level recorded verbatim). dancecentral charts change steps by transition: this is the NATURAL-TO-REVERSE backward change (Man LF back), matching the assignment rule; the REVERSE-TO-NATURAL backward change (Man RF back, turning 1/8 L, sways mirrored) is on .../viennese-waltz/reverse-to-natural (already charted as twin 'rf-backward-change-step-reverse-to-natural'). The page prints this chart with the Lady as primary dancer ('Lady/Man'); alignments/cells are role-relative and identical for the Man dancing it. Step 1 turn printed '1/8 to R btwn preceding step and 1' -> made between the preceding figure and step 1, recorded on step 1. Step 3 rows print no turn cell. Step 1 rise for the backward-moving dancer printed 'rise e/o1, NFR' -> recorded commence, NFR noted (backward partner has no foot rise); the forward-moving partner prints 'rise e/o 1'. No position column printed -> position omitted (closed hold implied but unprinted). Rise from the leader's (backward) column: commence / 'up on 2' / 'up on 3, lower e/o3' -> lowering.

figures.source: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/viennese-waltz/natural-to-reverse
figures.corroboration: Transcribed cell-by-cell from the dancecentral.info 'Natural To Reverse' chart (raw HTML fetched): Man dances RF Forward Change Step, Lady dances LF Backward Change Step. Matches the already-charted WDSF twin 'rf-forward-change-step-natural-to-reverse' (fwd HT / diag fwd T / close TH, 1/8 R per turning step, entry facing LOD, exit facing DW); dancecentral additionally prints an 1/8 R turn between the preceding step and step 1.
figures.note: Source labels this a Bronze figure (assignment lists ISTD Silver; source's level recorded verbatim). dancecentral charts change steps by transition, not as a generic Forward Change page: this is the NATURAL-TO-REVERSE forward change (Man RF fwd), the page's primary chart, per assignment rule; the REVERSE-TO-NATURAL forward change (Man LF fwd, turning 1/8 L, sways mirrored) is charted on .../viennese-waltz/reverse-to-natural (already charted as twin 'lf-forward-change-step-reverse-to-natural'). Step 1 turn printed '1/8 to R btwn preceding step and 1' -> turn made between the preceding figure and step 1, recorded on step 1 ('Start with Man facing DC, turning 1/8 to step RF forward along LOD'). Step 3 rows print no turn cell. Lady step 1 rise printed 'rise e/o1, NFR' (Man: 'rise e/o 1') -> Lady NFR noted. Page note: after the first half of a Natural Turn the Man may instead dance the LF Backward Change Step while the Lady dances the RF Forward Change. No position column printed on any row -> position omitted (closed hold implied but unprinted). Rise from Man's column: 'rise e/o 1' -> commence, 'up on 2' -> up, 'up on 3, lower e/o 3' -> lowering.

Verifier: 'Lower e/o 4' means end of this figure's step 4 (count 3) — the figure lowers at its own end, not on the following figure's first step. figures.source: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/waltz/waltz-closed-changes
figures.corroboration: Verbatim HTML transcription of the 'Waltz RF Closed Change (Natural to Reverse)' chart on the dancecentral Closed Changes page (the page's one fully detailed chart; the LF change is given only as 'the normal opposite' commencing DW). Equivalent to already-charted twins 'closed-change-on-rf' (this chart) / 'closed-change-on-lf' (the mirrored one).
figures.note: ISTD Bronze (page says Newcomer). No turn on any step ('Change step foot has no turn, but body has rotation'; 'a very slight turn to R may be made' — not charted as a value). Couple position is not printed anywhere on the page, so position omitted on all rows (standard closed position is implied by the figure name but not verifiable from the source). Man's step 3 alignment printed 'FD' — presumed typo for FDC (Man faces DC on steps 1-2 and Lady backs DC on all three steps); exitAlignment recorded as facing DC on that corroboration. Rise (Man): 1 'Start to rise at end of 1' -> commence; 2 'Cont to rise on 2 and 3' -> continue; 3 'Lower at end of 3' -> lowering. Lady's step 1 rise printed 'Start to rise e/o 1, NFR' (No Foot Rise — differs from Man). Both roles printed 'slight CBM' on step 1. Side steps printed 'side, slightly fwd' (Man) / 'side, slightly back' (Lady) -> mapped to side. Verifier: the LF Closed Change (Reverse to Natural) is actually printed as a complete chart on the page too (both roles, Man FDW / Lady BDW throughout, mirrored sways), not merely described as 'the normal opposite' — only the coaching commentary is unique to the RF chart. The transcribed RF (Natural to Reverse) chart matches the source cell-for-cell, including the 'FD' step-3 alignment typo and the no-turn-column layout.

figures.source: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/waltz/closed-impetus
figures.corroboration: Verbatim HTML transcription of the dancecentral Waltz Closed Impetus chart, cross-checked against a WebFetch pass that agreed on every cell (placements, alignments, 3/8 + 1/4 turn split, sway S/L/S vs S/R/S, footwork TH-HT-TH / HT-T-TH). Equivalent to the already-charted twin 'impetus'. Page states enter and exit in closed position, supporting position=closed on all rows.
figures.note: ISTD Bronze. Step 1 turn column printed 'Start to turn R' (no amount) for both roles — amount is charted where printed: 3/8 between 1-2 on step 2, 1/4 between 2-3 on step 3. Step 2 is the Man's Heel Turn (RF closes to LF); Lady steps side on 2 and 'RF diag fwd having brushed to LF' on 3 (brush noted). Rise (Man): step 1 rise cell printed '--' so omitted; step 2 printed 'Rise e/o 2' -> commence (late rise for the heel turn); step 3 printed 'Up. Lower e/o 3' -> lowering. Step 1 may be OP depending on the preceding figure (charted closed per the page's enter/exit statement).

figures.source: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/waltz/closed-telemark
figures.corroboration: Verbatim HTML transcription of the dancecentral Waltz Closed Telemark chart. Equivalent to the already-charted twin 'telemark'. The chart's trailing 'Count 1' rows are the first step of the FOLLOWING figure (Man RF fwd in CBMP OP) and are excluded from the 3 charted steps.
figures.note: ISTD Silver. Turn: step 1 printed 'start to turn L' (no amount, both roles); Man step 2 printed 'slightly less than 3/8 between 1-2' -> three_eighth_L (flagged: slightly less); Man step 3 printed 'slightly over 3/8 between 2-3. body turns less' -> three_eighth_L (flagged: slightly over; 'body turns less' kept as note per rules); Lady 3/8 between 1-2 and '3/8 between 2-3, body turns less'. Man's step 2 alignment printed 'almost BLOD' (backing LOD, flagged 'almost'); step 3 alignment 'Pointing DW'. Rise (Man): 1 'rise e/o 1' -> commence; 2 'Up' -> up; 3 'Up, lower e/o 3' -> lowering. Lady's rise on 1 printed 'rise slightly e/o 1, NFR' (No Foot Rise — differs from Man). Lady step 2 is a Heel Turn (LF closes to RF, footwork HT). Couple position not printed on any row, so position omitted (the following step, not charted here, is in CBMP OP). Verifier: Lady's printed rise also differs from the Man's on step 2 — 'continue to rise on 2' vs the Man's 'Up' (in addition to her step-1 'rise slightly e/o 1, NFR' already noted). Couple-level rise cells follow the Man's column as stated.

figures.source: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/waltz/closed-wing
figures.corroboration: Verbatim HTML transcription of the dancecentral Waltz Closed Wing chart. Equivalent to the already-charted twin 'wing'. Man stays facing DC on all three steps; Lady walks around him BDC -> BDC -> FDW against LOD into Wing position (the source explicitly names 'Wing position' on her step 3).
figures.note: ISTD Gold. Man's step 3 is placed WITHOUT weight ('LF closes to RF w/o weight'). Man's step 2 placement printed 'LF starts to close to RF' -> close (flagged: 'starts to close'; completes without weight on 3). Man's footwork on 2 and 3 kept verbatim: 'WF, pressure on IE of T of LF'. Turn: no amounts printed — Man printed 'Slight body turn to L on 2 and 3' and Lady's step 3 'Slight body turn to L' are body turns, kept as note per rules, no turn values emitted. Rise (Man's column): step 1 printed '-' so omitted; step 2 printed 'Slight rise on 2 and 3 NFR' -> body_rise (slight, No Foot Rise — verbatim kept here); step 3 '-' so omitted. Lady's rise differs materially: 1 'start to rise e/o 1, NFR', 2 'continue to rise on 2', 3 'Up. Lower e/o 3'. Sway: Man's sway cells all '-' (omitted); Lady printed 'Slight sway to L on 2 and 3' -> to_L on 2 and 3 (flagged: slight). No CBM marked on any step. Position: step 1 printed CBMP OP -> outside_partner; step 2 unprinted -> omitted; step 3 -> wing (source: Lady's 'L leg goes ahead of body to Wing position', 'LF fwd in CBMP, OP on L side').

Aggregate turns are printed as figure spans, not per-step: leader '3/4 to R between steps 2-6' (row 2) and follower '7/8 to R between steps 3 to 6' (row 3) are NOT recorded as step values because the source gives no per-step distribution; only the follower's '1/8 to L between steps 2-3' is discrete (recorded on step 3). Step 4: leader fwd in CBMP OP (outside partner), with printed note 'A sharp body turn to R is used on this step'. Step 5: FOLLOWER is the one stepping outside partner ('RF fwd in CBMP, OP'); row charted outside_partner accordingly. Step 6: follower's RF is placed to side WITHOUT WEIGHT in PP; footwork printed 'BH and IE of B of LF' (leader) / 'BH and IE of B of RF' (follower); follower alignment 'Backing DC, ending facing DC against LOD'. Row 3 has no position printed. Verified against the source 2026-07-15: all six rows match for both roles, including the role-asymmetric outside-partner steps (leader OP on step 4, follower OP on step 5), the aggregate turns left unrecorded (leader '3/4 to R between steps 2-6', follower '7/8 to R between steps 3 to 6') with only the follower's discrete '1/8 to L between steps 2-3' recorded on step 3, and the follower's step-6 RF placed to side without weight.

One per-step chart is given for Waltz/Foxtrot/Quickstep/Tango with per-dance timing. NOT corroborated by a WDSF/ISTD technique book (open, non-syllabus figure).
figures.note: Same Foot Lunge (4-step; both end extended on the SAME foot). Lady has a HEEL TURN on step 2. Man rises to full on 2 then lowers into the line (3-4); Lady dances with no rise. Turn charted from source fractions (Man 3/8+3/8 R; Lady 3/8 heel-turn then 1/2 R); step-4 'body turns to L' omitted (unquantified). Man sway 'S R S, L to R' (step 4 charted to_R, a L->R transition); Lady 'S S S slightly L'. Head weights not modeled. TANGO: rise omitted; sway none on 1-3 per source 'not to rise or swing into 1-2'. Source timings vary and hold the line; charted a faithful 4-count timing per dance (see start/finish notes). RISE IS OMITTED from this chart: the figure's rise is asymmetric (Man rises to full on 2 then lowers into the line; Lady dances with no rise, taking a heel turn on 2) and the chart's rise field is couple-level (shared), so it cannot faithfully represent the two roles separately — omitted rather than mis-shared.
